title: linum tenuifolium subsp. appressum
intro: Linum tenuifolium subsp. appressum, also known as purple flax or blue flax, is a subspecies of the Linum tenuifolium plant, a flowering plant in the flax family (Linaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin issues. The health benefits of Linum tenuifolium subsp. appressum include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and possibly providing antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying the leaves and stems, and have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, although its effectiveness and safety should be further researched and verified by medical professionals.
